channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Claims Interception of 339 Ukrainian Drones Over 13 Hours
item.description: Russian Defence Ministry reports its air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones across multiple regions including Moscow in a 13-hour period, marking one of the largest drone attacks on Russian territory.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Kill 12 Including Senior Military Officers
item.description: Israeli attacks in Lebanon killed 12 people including a brigadier general and captain, escalating tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border amid ongoing regional conflict.

item.title: ⛽ US Energy Secretary: Lower Gas Prices Require Resolution With Iran
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that lowering pump prices will ultimately require a resolution with Iran to restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, linking domestic energy costs to the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba's Raul Castro Makes First Public Appearance Since US Indictment
item.description: Former Cuban leader Raul Castro appeared at an Interior Ministry event in Havana, his first public appearance since being indicted on murder charges in the United States last month.

item.title: 🇳🇿 US Approves .5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
item.description: The United States approved the sale of five maritime helicopters to New Zealand worth .5 billion, as Wellington moves to nearly double its military spending amid growing regional security concerns.

item.title: ☢️ Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power after outage
item.description: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its power supply on Saturday after an outage raised safety concerns, according to the facility's Russian-installed management.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin signals openness to new U.S. relationship amid Ukraine stalemate
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has indicated potential for a renewed relationship with the United States, even as negotiations to end the Ukraine war remain deadlocked and Moscow faces mounting challenges on the battlefield.

item.title: ⚰️ Seven-month-old Palestinian baby buried after Israeli forces shooting
item.description: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old Palestinian baby boy who was killed when Israeli troops fired at the vehicle he was in during a military operation in the occupied West Bank, according to medical officials.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian students protest nationwide over exam changes, arrests reported
item.description: Students across Iran demonstrate against changes to university entrance exams, chanting slogans demanding justice and education rights. First significant domestic protests since the US-Iran conflict began.

item.title: 🌍 WHO warns of nearly 500 Ebola cases in Central Africa outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization reports 100 new cases and 20 additional deaths in the Central Africa Ebola outbreak, raising alarm about the epidemic scale. Cases rose from 400 to nearly 500 in one week.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan to designate Philippines as top priority for oil reserve support
item.description: Tokyo plans to work with JICA starting this summer to provide oil reserve support to the Philippines, deepening energy cooperation amid regional tensions with China in the South China Sea.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Nancy Mace breaks with Trump over Epstein vote in heated primary battle
item.description: Congresswoman Nancy Mace faces consequences from Trump for her vote on Jeffrey Epstein-related legislation as she navigates a contentious primary challenge. The vote has become a flashpoint in her re-election campaign.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇩🇪 Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schroeder
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder in a one-on-one meeting described as "good and friendly" by the Kremlin, amid ongoing tensions over the war in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Senior US Diplomat Urges Taiwan to Spend Smarter on Defense
item.description: Taiwan needs to "spend smarter" on defense and learn from the Ukraine and Middle East wars on using drones to counter China, the de facto U.S. ambassador to Taipei said.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance in Congress
item.description: Republicans and Democrats express skepticism about a revolt against Trump, but greater willingness to break with him could threaten his ambitious initiatives before the midterms.

item.title: 🤖 White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
item.description: White House AI policy adviser Krishnan is leaving his position, according to information reports, at a time of increasing focus on artificial intelligence policy.

item.title: 🏛️ Defense Secretary Hegseth Links Immigration to Invasion in D-Day Speech
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech in France to draw parallels between WWII and current immigration challenges, calling immigration by sea an invasion that threatens nations.

item.title: 📉 US Stocks Tumble After Strong Jobs Data Signals Higher Rates
item.description: The S&P 500 fell more than 2.6% on Friday, its worst one-day drop of the year. An unexpectedly strong May jobs report bolstered bets that US interest rates are poised to rise, knocking tech stocks lower.

item.title: 🇺🇸 100 Days Into Iran War, Trump Struggles to Rally Domestic Support
item.description: As the US-Iran conflict reaches the 100-day mark, analysts note growing war weariness among Americans. The unpopularity of the conflict may impact Republican electoral prospects and US policy decisions.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran Launches Ballistic Missiles Toward Gulf Allies as US Intercepts
item.description: Iran fired ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain, with US forces intercepting most of them. The attack marks another escalation in the ongoing US-Iran conflict despite ceasefire talks.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Toward Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after Iran launched four attack drones toward the Strait of Hormuz. The US military shot down all drones, calling the strike a defensive action amid fragile ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistani Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Iran-US Mediation Efforts
item.description: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to mediate between Iran and the United States as tensions continue to escalate in the Gulf region.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Vows to Accelerate Military Buildup Citing Russian Threat
item.description: Germany pledged to speed up its military expansion efforts, citing ongoing security concerns related to Russia as the driving factor behind the accelerated defense spending.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Condemns Israeli Aggression Against Lebanon
item.description: Saudi Arabia issued a strong condemnation of what it called Israeli aggression against Lebanon, adding regional pressure amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.

item.title: 🇦🇱 Albanians Protest Jared Kushner-Backed Luxury Resort on Nature Reserve
item.description: Thousands of Albanians protested plans for a luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner on the country's coast, raising concerns about environmental impact on protected areas.
